title: Merge Intervals
description: Given a collection of intervals, merge all overlapping intervals into one and return a new collection of intervals. Each interval is represented as a pair of integers, where the first integer is the start time and the second integer is the end time.

examples.explanation: The intervals [1,3] and [2,6] overlap, so they are merged to become [1,6]. The intervals [8,10] and [15,18] do not overlap with any other intervals, so they remain unchanged.
The intervals [1,4] and [4,5] share a boundary, thus they can be merged into a single interval [1,5].
The intervals [1,4] and [0,4] overlap completely; hence, they merge into [0,4].

constraints: The input intervals array will have at most 10^4 elements.
Each interval will be a list of two integers where start ≤ end.
hints: Consider sorting the intervals based on the starting times.
Use a loop to compare the current interval with the last added interval in the result.
Think about how to handle merging when intervals overlap.
What happens when the intervals are completely separate?
